reaches.toDesc: Table Mountain bicycle bridge in Oroville

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(43) — Feather River below Fish Barrier Dam (Butte, Sutter and Yuba cos.)
sources.quotedText: All year. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead in possession.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 223423, "Feather River", Stream, Butte/Sutter/Yuba cos., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":223423,"gaz_name":"Feather River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Butte/Sutter/Yuba"} — geometry multipoint spanning the whole named stream from its mouth near Verona (lon -121.6214, lat 38.7855) up through Butte, Sutter and Yuba Cos. to its northernmost vertex near Oroville (lon -121.4872, lat 39.5338). This entry covers only the sub-reach below Fish Barrier Dam, so the boundary-defining feature (the dam itself) is used as the representative point rather than the river's mouth: the Fish Barrier Dam sits at approximately lat 39°31'18" N, lon 121°32'48" W (NAD27) per public references to its as-built location, and the co-located USGS streamgage 11407000 "FEATHER R A OROVILLE CA" (immediately below the dam) reports lat 39.52155294, lon -121.5477477 (NAD83) — both confirming the file's existing water coordinate (-121.5467, 39.5217), which sits within ~100 m of the gauge.

parameters.note: Fish Barrier Dam holds anadromous fish below it for hatchery operations; the reach immediately downstream to the Table Mountain bicycle bridge is closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year from Fish Barrier Dam to the Table Mountain bicycle bridge in Oroville.
regulations.verbatimText: From Fish Barrier Dam to Table Mountain bicycle bridge in Oroville. Closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Open January 1 through July 15 from the Table Mountain bicycle bridge to the Highway 70 bridge (closed Jul. 16 - Dec. 31).
regulations.verbatimText: From Table Mountain bicycle bridge to Highway 70 bridge. Jan. 1 through Jul. 15.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used from the Table Mountain bicycle bridge to the Highway 70 bridge.
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Limit applies to hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead only (denoted by ** in the regulation, i.e. fish with a healed adipose fin clip). Wild, unclipped trout and steelhead are not part of this bag limit.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily bag limit of 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead; 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year from the Highway 70 bridge to the unimproved boat ramp above the Thermalito Afterbay Outfall.
regulations.verbatimText: From Highway 70 bridge to the unimproved boat ramp above the Thermalito Afterbay Outfall. All year.

parameters.counting_rule: Limit applies to hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead only (denoted by ** in the regulation, i.e. fish with a healed adipose fin clip). Wild, unclipped trout and steelhead are not part of this bag limit.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily bag limit of 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead; 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: The source table lists this reach in three consecutive date ranges, each carrying the same open status and 2/4 hatchery trout-steelhead bag limit; combined they amount to a year-round open season, interrupted only by the mid-year Chinook salmon closure noted separately.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year from the unimproved boat ramp above the Thermalito Afterbay Outfall to 200 yards above the Live Oak boat ramp (listed in the regulation as three back-to-back date ranges).
regulations.verbatimText: From the unimproved boat ramp above the Thermalito Afterbay Outfall to 200 yards above the Live Oak boat ramp. Jan. 1 through Jul. 15. ... Jul. 16 through Oct. 31. ... Nov. 1 through Dec. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: Limit applies to hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead only (denoted by ** in the regulation, i.e. fish with a healed adipose fin clip). Wild, unclipped trout and steelhead are not part of this bag limit. Applies year-round on this reach.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily bag limit of 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead; 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Chinook salmon take and possession is closed from July 16 through October 31, even though the reach otherwise remains open to fishing for hatchery trout/steelhead.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to the take and possession of Chinook salmon from July 16 through October 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to the take and possession of Chinook Salmon. (Jul. 16 through Oct. 31)

parameters.note: The source table lists this reach in three consecutive date ranges, each carrying the same open status and 2/4 hatchery trout-steelhead bag limit; combined they amount to a year-round open season, interrupted only by the mid-year Chinook salmon closure noted separately.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year from 200 yards above the Live Oak boat ramp to the mouth (a line from the peninsula point on the west bank to the Verona Marine boat ramp), listed in the regulation as three back-to-back date ranges.
regulations.verbatimText: From 200 yards above Live Oak boat ramp to the mouth. For purposes of this subsection, the lower boundary is defined as a straight line drawn from the peninsula point on the west bank to the Verona Marine boat ramp. Jan. 1 through Jul. 15. ... Jul. 16 through Dec. 16. ... Dec. 17 through Dec. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: Limit applies to hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead only (denoted by ** in the regulation, i.e. fish with a healed adipose fin clip). Wild, unclipped trout and steelhead are not part of this bag limit. Applies year-round on this reach.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily bag limit of 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead; 4 in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Chinook salmon take and possession is closed from July 16 through December 16, even though the reach otherwise remains open to fishing for hatchery trout/steelhead.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to the take and possession of Chinook salmon from July 16 through December 16.
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to the take and possession of Chinook Salmon. (Jul. 16 through Dec. 16)

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
